title: Detandra paraensis
intro: Detandra paraensis, also known as Para Detandra, is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae, which is native to the tropical forests of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, as well as its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Detandra paraensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Detandra paraensis is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions, including coughs, colds, and bronchitis.
taxonomy: Detandra paraensis is classified under the family Meliaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the mahogany family. The genus Detandra is a part of the Meliaceae family, which consists of about 150 species of trees and shrubs. These plants are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Within the Detandra genus, Detandra paraensis is a specific species that has been identified and classified based on its unique characteristics. The classification of Detandra paraensis is an important tool for botanists and researchers studying the plant's behavior and characteristics.
